title: Biryani, secret marriage, divorce, murder: Twists and turns in Bengaluru child death
content: Kadugodi police have launched a murder investigation into the death of six-year-old Vennela, with her mother, advocate P Priyanka, and her friend Mohan G Mahalingappa booked for murder. The child's father alleges they killed Vennela to be together, with inconsistencies in the mother's account raising suspicions. Mohan has been remanded to custody, while Priyanka remains untraceable.

title: INDIA bloc huddle in Delhi: TMC among 23 parties to attend June 8 meet; DMK, AAP may skip
content: INDIA bloc leaders convene in Delhi today amidst recent election defeats and internal friction. Twenty-three parties confirmed participation, aiming to present a united front against the BJP. However, key allies like DMK and AAP are reportedly skipping the meeting due to ongoing disputes, testing the coalition's resolve.

title: 100 days on: Why is Ali Khamenei still unburied?
content: Over 100 days after his death in a US-Israel strike, Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has not been buried. The prolonged delay, attributed to security concerns for his successor Mojtaba Khamenei and potential damage to the remains, has fueled speculation and left the nation in a state of confusion regarding the funeral and leadership transition.

title: Manav Suthar stars on debut as India leave Afghanistan reeling in one-off Test
content: India dominated day two of the Test against Afghanistan. Hosts declared at 564/8, with centuries from Shubman Gill and KL Rahul. Debutant Manav Suthar impressed with three wickets. Afghanistan ended the day at 113/5, trailing by 451 runs. Rahmat Shah reached 1,000 Test runs. India is in a strong position to win.

title: Watch: Mob thrashes and strips Dum Dum TMC councillor over ‘theft of relief material’
content: Trinamool councillor Shankar Das was severely beaten and stripped by a mob at his Kolkata home, allegedly over land-grabbing accusations. The incident followed the discovery of relief materials at a business owned by the municipality chairman. Two associates of the chairman were also attacked. Police have arrested two individuals and are investigating the pre-planned conspiracy claims.

title: Jeff Bezos wants people to stop blaming Airbnb for high rents in New York city
content: Jeff Bezos argued on CNBC that government policies limiting housing supply, not Airbnb, are the primary drivers of high rents in New York City. He cited zoning and permitting restrictions as key factors, stating that subsidizing demand while constraining supply inevitably leads to soaring prices. Bezos also criticized corporate welfare and special tax provisions as forms of crony capitalism.

title: 'Protest won't stop until Pradhan quits,' says CJP founder, security beefed up at his house
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) founder Abhijeet Dipke vows to continue protests until Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns. Following a large demonstration at Jantar Mantar, Dipke announced plans to expand the agitation nationwide, demanding accountability for alleged examination and recruitment irregularities. Security has been heightened at Dipke's residence.

title: 'Next shot, in your chest': Bishnoi gang claims Portugal shooting targeting Indians
content: The Lawrence Bishnoi gang has claimed responsibility for two recent shootings in Portugal targeting Indian nationals. A social media post, attributed to a member of the Sunil Meena Gang, detailed attacks in Vila Nova de Milfontes and Sintra, issuing lethal threats and warning of further escalation.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
content: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

title: CBSE students demand grace marks, fee waiver over evaluation errors
content: Thousands of CBSE Class 12 students are demanding grace marks and fee waivers for verification and re-evaluation. They cite technical glitches in the new On-Screen Marking system, leading to low scores and delayed answer sheet access, jeopardizing admissions. Students argue they shouldn't pay for errors originating from the evaluation process itself, impacting their future prospects.

title: Too proud to deal? Why Iran is yet to agree to US negotiation - Trump explains
content: President Trump stated Iran has not yet agreed to a deal to end the ongoing conflict, citing leadership pride and resolve. He believes Tehran will eventually concede, as they've faced insufficient consequences for decades.

title: Quote of the day by ‘Game of Thrones’ actor Peter Dinklage: ‘I have a friend who says...'
content: Actor Peter Dinklage, known for his role in 'Game of Thrones,' shared a quote about not needing 'another angry dwarf.' He used his 2012 Golden Globe acceptance speech to highlight a hate crime against a British dwarf, demonstrating that impactful change can be achieved through thoughtful action rather than constant outrage.

title: Elon Musk reacts to post claiming India's birth rate falls below replacement level
content: Elon Musk reacted to a viral X post claiming India's fertility rate has fallen below replacement level for the first time. The post highlighted a decline from 2.3 to 1.9 in a decade, with Delhi's rate at 1.2. Musk, a vocal critic of declining birth rates, noted this trend, particularly among the educated.

title: Chernobyl fuel facility hit: Nuclear fears resurface, Kyiv says Russian attack ‘deliberate'
content: A Russian drone struck a spent nuclear fuel storage facility in Ukraine's Chornobyl exclusion zone, officials reported. While authorities confirmed radiation levels remained normal, President Zelenskyy condemned the attack as a deliberate act of recklessness targeting critical infrastructure. This incident adds to a pattern of Russian actions endangering nuclear safety, with Kyiv accusing Moscow of systemic nuclear blackmail.

title: The rise of Mohammad Saleem Safi: Quit cricket at 16, now bags 6/140 vs India
content: Three years after catching Rana Naved-ul-Hasan's attention at Afghanistan's high-performance centre, Mohammad Saleem Safi delivered the performance of his life against India. The 23-year-old pacer claimed a maiden six-wicket haul, completing a remarkable journey from financial hardship and self-doubt to becoming Afghanistan's newest pace sensation.

title: Another man dies of suspected drug overdose at Mumbai concert
content: A 28-year-old man died after falling ill at an overnight music concert in Mumbai's Worli. Vrishabh Mahendra Gangurde was attending the 'Klangkuenstler All Night Long' event when he and a female companion experienced health issues. Police are investigating the circumstances, including potential alcohol or substance consumption, and awaiting post-mortem results.

title: Patrick Mahomes & Brittany Mahomes net worth 2026: Earnings & lifestyle
content: Patrick Mahomes is embracing his quick recovery by enjoying sports with wife Brittany Mahomes. Their shared passion for sports extends to investments in various franchises. They co-own the Kansas City Current. The couple has been high school sweethearts. They have built a life together with three children and a luxurious Missouri mansion.

title: Manav Suthar's dream debut: First Indian in 25 years to ...
content: Manav Suthar made a dream debut for India, taking a wicket on the fourth ball of his Test career against Afghanistan. The young left-arm spinner became the eighth Indian to achieve this feat, joining an elite list of bowlers. His immediate impact justified the team's faith in his selection.

title: Whodunit? 100 Surat homes demolished but no one knows who razed them
content: Over 100 homes were mysteriously razed in Surat's Nashir Nagar, sparking a major controversy. The Surat Municipal Corporation denies any demolition orders, yet officials and police were present. Authorities are investigating this 'ghost demolition,' allegedly to clear land for a private road project, with local MLA demanding strict action.

title: Ctrl+alt+examine: Can India's biggest exams go online?
content: India's massive entrance exam system faces a digital dilemma. While online tests promise cost savings, speed, and reduced environmental impact, significant digital divides in infrastructure and familiarity pose major hurdles. Ensuring equitable access and preparation for all students remains paramount before a widespread shift can be considered successful.

title: 'Peddi' row: Janhvi's alleged chats mention Sreeleela, spark buzz
content: Alleged private messages attributed to Janhvi Kapoor have surfaced online, reigniting discussions around the film 'Peddi'. The unverified screenshots suggest the actress had concerns about her role and the filming process, even mentioning her father's potential presence. These revelations follow director Buchi Babu Sana's defense of the movie's portrayal of women.

title: Gurgaon shocker: School principal, 25-year-old son shot dead by husband
content: A 45-year-old school principal and her 25-year-old son were fatally shot by the woman's husband in Gurgaon following a family dispute. The accused, a security officer, was detained by police after the incident. An investigation is underway to determine the exact motive behind the tragic deaths.

title: 'We're children of the same rivers': Nepal calls on India to 'discuss dormant ties'
content: Nepal's Foreign Minister Shisir Khanal on Sunday called for a stronger development-oriented partnership with India, stressing on the revival of dormant bilateral mechanisms and high-level political exchanges to resolve issues such around boundary. During his visit, discussions covered trade, connectivity, energy, water resources, and people-to-people ties, with a focus on economic growth and innovation.

title: UP cops more loyal to governing party than Constitution, says Allahabad HC
content: The Allahabad High Court observed that Uttar Pradesh police officers exhibit loyalty to the ruling party over the Constitution, hindering constitutional governance. The court noted that transfers, postings, and promotions are often based on political patronage rather than merit, leading officers to prioritize political superiors' satisfaction over upholding the rule of law and due process.

title: Gunshots, screams & panic - Ohio mass shooting caught on cam, several injured
content: Panic erupted near Toledo's Old West End Festival on Saturday as gunfire injured at least 12 people, with two in critical condition. Authorities suspect multiple shooters, possibly exchanging fire. The incident, which saw festival-goers fleeing for safety, is under ongoing investigation. Governor DeWine emphasized the need for safe community events.

title: Quote of the day by Cate Blanchett
content: Known as a luminary of modern cinema, Cate Blanchett personifies the reality that influential women often encounter doubt at their inception. Her illustrious career, packed with bold performances and critical accolades, parallels the path of Katharine Hepburn. Blanchett’s journey illustrates that a woman’s straightforwardness is often misinterpreted, signaling that apprehension towards the unfamiliar is merely a precursor to transformative moments.

title: ‘Dropped off by one son ...’: Indian mother's journey with her pilot sons touches hearts
content: A mother's travel plans took a heartwarming turn when her two pilot sons orchestrated a special reunion. One son saw her off at the airport, while the other, as captain, welcomed her aboard her flight. This touching family moment, shared online, has captivated social media users, who lauded the brothers' thoughtful gesture and the mother's evident pride.

title: Only Bradman ahead: Gill enters elite Test captaincy list with another century
content: Shubman Gill is experiencing a career-defining period as India's Test captain, transforming into a prolific run-scorer. His recent century against Afghanistan highlights his exceptional record, boasting 1,076 runs in 15 innings at an average of 82.76. Gill's captaincy has seen him achieve significant milestones, including becoming the second-fastest Indian captain to reach 1,000 Test runs.

title: ‘Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai’ Box office day 3: Eyes Rs 25 crore worldwide
content: Varun Dhawan's latest film, 'Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai', marks a poignant finale to his collaboration with father David Dhawan. Starring Pooja Hegde and Mrunal Thakur, the successful venture has impressed audiences, amassing over Rs 15.59 crore in India by its third day. Despite stiff competition, the film has raked in a remarkable worldwide gross of Rs 23.20 crore. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 'Umpiring decisions go in their favour': Pakistan's Khushdil alleges bias towards India in cricket
content: Pakistan all-rounder Khushdil Shah alleges that India frequently benefits from favorable umpiring and match-related decisions in India-Pakistan encounters. Speaking on the ARY Podcast, Shah claimed that decisions on and off the field, including those in the dressing room and match scheduling, often favor the Men in Blue.

title: 'He is a kid': BCCI to bear parents' expenses as Sooryavanshi begins India journey
content: The BCCI will cover all expenses for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents to accompany the 15-year-old on upcoming Ireland and England tours. This move aims to ease the teenage batting prodigy's transition into the senior Indian team environment after a stellar IPL 2026. The board prioritises his comfort and adjustment to international cricket.

title: Albania’s luxury coastal plan linked to Trump sparks storm - why are people protesting?
content: Thousands of Albanians are protesting a major coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner, fearing the handover of untouched coastline to powerful investors. The scheme, planned for a wildlife reserve and a former military island, has drawn criticism from environmental groups and an anti-corruption investigation. Prime Minister Edi Rama defends the project as crucial for tourism and EU aspirations.

title: Maharashtra onion farmers demand Rs 3,000/quintal, say relief measures fall short
content: Maharashtra farmers welcomed relaxed onion procurement rules but demand a minimum support price of Rs 3,000 per quintal, stating current rates of Rs 1,580 are below production costs. They seek transparent procurement and compensation for past losses, arguing that rule changes alone won't resolve their financial distress.

title: 'Armed with bamboo sticks': RJD workers step in after Lalu, Rabri lose Z+ security
content: Bihar's political landscape is stirred as Lalu Prasad, Rabri Devi, and Tejashwi Yadav returned security personnel after their cover was downgraded. RJD workers have now taken over guarding Rabri Devi's residence, citing a "bold decision" against perceived political vendetta and insults from the government.

title: 'This doesn't end here': Abhijeet Dipke outlines the next move for CJP
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has issued a seven-day ultimatum to the government, demanding the removal or resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. This follows a large protest at Jantar Mantar, where thousands voiced anger over alleged examination and recruitment irregularities. CJP vows nationwide protests if their demands aren't met.

title: Banned hookah, ganja, liquor: 156 detained in Pune late-night party raid
content: Pune police raided a party in Tulapur, detaining 156 individuals and seizing liquor worth Rs 9.22 lakh. Three minors were found present, and the event exceeded its permit. Organizers Aiman Sheikh and Yash Chaudhary are identified. This action is part of a broader crackdown on illegal liquor activities following recent fatalities.

title: Malayalam actor Salim Kumar cremated with State honours
content: Funeral of the 57-year-old actor, who died due to cardiac arrest while undergoing treatment at a private hospital in Kochi on June 6, was conducted on the premises of Laughing Villa, his residence at Chittattukara in Ernakulam

title: NTA releases NEET-UG 2026 re-exam city intimation slip for June 21; admit card to be issued later
content: According to a public notice issued by the agency, the re-examination will be conducted in pen-and-paper mode from 2 pm to 5:15 p.m. across 551 cities in India and 14 cities abroad

title: LPG price hikes: Government will pay 'political price', says Sharad Pawar; Congress's Wadettiwar slams BJP
content: "The hike may have started with just two rupees, but over time the increases have kept piling up, and today we can see how far the prices have risen. All of this has a direct impact on the finances and daily lives of ordinary people," says Pawar

title: Jantar Mantar protest a trailer, Pradhan must resign in seven days: Cockroach Janta Party founder Dipke
content: Mr. Dipke also said their protest at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi on Saturday (June 6) was largely successful, with around 7,000 people participating in it, and added that the movement would now expand nationwide

title: Varanasi civic body approves shifting of meat, fish shops to city outskirts
content: The move is aimed at improving sanitation and streamlining the functioning of such markets while ensuring continued access to consumers

content: The INDIA bloc is set to deliberate on the future course of action with an eye on the 2029 general elections

title: T.N. government’s decision to shelve smart meters project a grave mistake: BJP
content: Smart meters would create a robust infrastructure fully supported by loans and grants from Central government agencies, along with private capital, Narayanan Thirupathy said

title: Indicators cited as ‘missing’ being monitored through national surveys, databases: Government sources
content: The objective is to ensure that each indicator is reported through the most appropriate and authoritative source, reducing duplication and improving overall data coherence, say Union Health Ministry sources

title: 535 farmers died due to unsafe pesticide use between January 2024-2026 in Rajasthan
content: The department stated that farmers, agricultural labourers and other persons may fall ill and, in some cases, die due to failure to adopt necessary safety measures during pesticide spraying and because of indiscriminate and unsafe use of pesticides

title: Mamata travels to Delhi to attend INDIA bloc meeting amid speculation of split among Trinamool MPs
content: The former West Bengal chief minister was accompanied by Rajya Sabha MP Dola Sen and veteran Lok Sabha MP Kalyan Banerjee

title: White Paper reopens debate on nuclear power plants in Kerala
content: The document lists nuclear power as one of the sources that the State should explore in adopting a “long-term strategy” for energy security

title: Minister Ramalinga Reddy’s resignation issue resolved, says Chief Minister Shivakumar
content: Mr. Shivakumar met Mr. Reddy for nearly two-and-a-half hours at a private hotel in Jayanagar late on June 5, 2026 night

title: Kerala government issues orders reinstating suspended IAS officers B. Ashok and N. Prasanth in service
content: Pransanth was placed under suspension in November, 2024, which has since been extended multiple times whereas Ashok had been suspended in April this year

title: Meghalaya village protests India-Bangladesh border fence alignment, fears isolation
content: We are not against border fencing, but we want the fence to be erected at the zero line so that our village remains inside India and within the fenced area, village head Ramu said

title: CM Satheesan remembers Salim Kumar as a brother as condolences pour in for actor
content: Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan said that he was at short of words to mourn Salim Kumar, who was like a member of his own family

title: Why aren’t BJP leaders taking to streets with cylinders in protest now: Congress’ dig on LPG price hike
content: Mallikarjun Kharge said Prime Minister Narendra Modi had made "grand claims" in Parliament about diversifying fuel sources across 41 countries in response to the West Asia conflict and asked what became of that

title: CPM criticises Andhra Pradesh Coalition Government on completion of two years
content: CPI(M) leaders accuse the Andhra Praadesh government of prioritising corporate interests over public welfare as they mark the two-year milestone in power

title: Rahul Devraj appointed NIMS Hyderabad Director for three-year term
content: Dr. Rahul Devraj is among the institute’s senior faculty members and has been associated with its clinical, academic and administrative activities for several years

title: Lack of privacy, toilets, persistent stigma forces girls in Odisha to miss school during menstruation
content: While 94% of the surveyed schools had separate toilets for girls, the lack of basic menstrual hygiene support systems, and non-availability of water and soap were cited as major hurdles

title: TVK Government drawing criticism well before six months, says Stalin
content: According to former T.N. Chief Minister Stalin, the government’s functioning had already drawn criticism from various quarters

title: Special Yoga event at Srisailam temple on Monday
content: All arrangements are being made to conduct the yoga event in the area stretching from the Gangadhara Mandapam to the Nandi Mandapam, temple Executive Officer said

title: Satish Jarkiholi says he is happy with his portfolio
content: “I never asked for any specific portfolio from the party high command or the Chief Minister,” says Satish Jarkiholi

title: Hyderabad man shot dead during pizza delivery in Philadelphia
content: According to information shared with them, he had been assigned a delivery to a deserted location which later appeared to have been a setup

title: Arrested TMC councillor faces public fury, egg attack
content: The Councillor and his associate were booked under multiple sections of the BNS, including charges related to extortion, criminal trespass, criminal intimidation, attempted arson and other serious offences

title: U.P. advances SDG-6 goals through integrated water management efforts
content: State reports decline in over-exploited groundwater units and rise in safe blocks as it pursues clean drinking water, sanitation and sustainable groundwater management

title: LPG price hike: Government says rates in India among world's lowest despite 46% jump in global benchmark
content: The government said the cost of supplying a domestic LPG cylinder has risen to more than ₹1,600 following a surge in international prices that followed the outbreak of war in West Asia at the end February

title: Chief Minister travels by Metro to avoid Zero-Traffic arrangements in Bengaluru
content: Shivakumar is visiting Kanakapura, his home turf, first time after becoming the Karnataka Chief Minister to meet and extend his gratitude to the people of his constituency

title: Ladakh L-G Saxena launches ecological restoration project to reclaim 800 acres of barren land
content: Lieutenant-Governor Vinai Kumar Saxena initiates an ecological restoration project to reclaim 800 acres of barren land in Ladakh

title: Ladakh L-G to lead Indian delegation to bring Buddha relics back from Mongolia
content: The visit comes in the wake of the successful exposition of the sacred Piprahwa relics of Lord Buddha in Ladakh from May 1 to 14 this year

title: Security beefed up at CJP founder Dipke's home in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar
content: Earlier, 11 security personnel were deployed; now the number has reached 15; the deployed staff is from the local police station as well as the city police headquarters, said an official from MIDC Waluj police station

title: Despite last-minute preparations, visitor numbers during summer festival season increased 28% in 2026
content: With the election results being declared in May and a new government taking charge, preparations for the annual shows — the Rose Show, Flower Show, Plantation Crops Show and Fruit Show, were delayed, officials said

title: CMDA holds stakeholder meeting on online building planning system
content: CMDA convenes stakeholders to enhance Chennai’s online building planning system, focusing on efficiency and user interface improvements

title: Andhra University to re-conduct B.Sc. Chemistry examination on June 9
content: Details of the revised timetable and examination centres have been made available on the university’s examinations website, exams.andhrauniversity.edu.in, the release said

title: NLCIL observes World Environment Day
content: NLCIL celebrated World Environment Day with sapling drives and climate awareness initiatives, themed “Inspired by Nature. For Climate. For Our Future”

title: Coast Guard launches search for missing fishermen off Cuddalore
content: Based on a request from the Cuddalore district administration, the Coast Guard has deployed two vessels and a helicopter to the area to look for the fishermen

title: Contempt plea moved against former Chennai Corporation Commissioner Kumaragurubaran
content: A disability rights activist accuses Kumaragurubaran of having wilfully disobeyed a court order for rectification of bollards that hinder free movement of wheelchairs

title: Telangana schools to reopen on June 15, not 12, clarifies School Education Department
content: However, the second Saturday next month (July 11) will be treated as a regular working day for all the schools

title: Survey in Rajasthan to scrutinise unauthorised constructions, funding in border areas
content: The exercise covers all constructions within 15 kilometres of the international border and follows directions issued by Union Home Minister Amit Shah during a high-level security review meeting in Bikaner last month

title: Trinamool's minority cell secretary Ajmal Siddiqui resigns, blames Abhishek Banerjee for party ‘collapse’
content: "The party is collapsing today solely because of one man: Abhishek Banerjee. His dictatorial attitude and the oppression we faced — false cases filed against us 12 or 13 years ago, demands for money — were unbearable," Ajmal Siddiqui said

title: LPG domestic cylinder now costs ₹957.50 in Chennai, consumers express shock
content: In a span of three months it has gone up by ₹89/14.2 kg cylinder. Customers who have already booked their refills but have not received the same, will have to pay the revised rate

title: Tamil Nadu Governor leads plantation drive, lays foundation stone for PLAZA in Auroville
content: The government formally announced that Auroville plans to plant more than 10,000 trees in the near future to further enrich its dense, sustainable urban forest

title: Congress aims for organisation overhaul, agitations from June 18 to corner BJP Govt in Goa
content: The reconstitution of the Goa Pradesh Congress Committee executive and other organisational bodies will soon be announced as part of a comprehensive restructuring exercise

title: Collector launches Yogandhra 2026 program
content: The ‘Yogandhra-2026’ program would be conducted over a period of 14 days, from June 7 leading up to International Yoga Day on June 21.

title: IDFC First Bank-AU Finance Bank fraud case: CBI conducts searches at six locations
content: The searches were carried out on Friday (June 5, 2026) as part of an ongoing probe into the alleged misappropriation of funds parked with IDFC First Bank and AU Finance Bank, an official statement said

title: CJP’s agitation to continue until Union minister Pradhan resigns: Abhijeet Dipke
content: The CJP, a youth-led online movement that has been demanding accountability for alleged irregularities in examinations and recruitment tests, including NEET, CBSE, CUET, and SSC examinations

title: Railways to invest ₹1 lakh crore in upgrading infrastructure in West Bengal
content: The Minister also said that the proposed bullet train services between Delhi–Varanasi and Varanasi–Siliguri were aimed at sharply reducing travel time

title: Telangana DCA issues notices to 41 online pharmacy-linked medical shops after statewide inspections
content: The inspections covered 166 medical shops, including 159 establishments associated with online medicine delivery services and 7 shops linked to online business-to-business (B2B) platforms such as IndiaMART

content: Accompanied by Indonesia’s lower house Representative Marlyn Maisarah, Sugionothe Foreign Minister is leading a high-level delegation to the national capital

title: Domestic LPG price hiked by ₹29 per 14.2-kg cylinder
content: The increase follows a ₹60-per-cylinder hike on March 7 after the conflict in West Asia disrupted global energy supplies and drove up international fuel prices.

content: The former BJP Tamil Nadu president has quit the party and launched a new ‘movement’, promising to bring ‘constructive’ and ‘clean’ politics

content: The complaint alleged trees were illegally uprooted during the activity, which amounted to violation of forest laws and government policies aimed at increasing green cover

content: The incident had been wrongly linked by some social media users to the so-called 'Khoda incident' in neighbouring Ghaziabad, where a 17-year-old Surya Pratap Chauhan was stabbed to death.

content: Union Minister Jitendra Singh said the lavender festival in Jammu and Kashmir's Bhaderwah valley has transformed the region into a national hub of lavender cultivation and aroma-based entrepreneurship

content: The statement came a day before Chinese President Xi Jinping visits North Korea for talks with Kim Jong Un, in his first visit to the country in seven years.

content: Israel's police said they had killed one attacker. Large cohorts of Israeli soldiers carried out searches in the area, on the ground and by air, for additional assailants.

title: Not Just Bullet Train, Centre Has Big Railway Development Plans For Bengal
content: Projects worth Rs 1 lakh crore will be undertaken for Bengal, the railway minister said, stating that the funds are ready.

content: The man and the woman attended the 'Klangkuenstler All Night Long' music event at the NSCI Dome Worli late last night.

content: The Kerala Chief Minister stressed that the issue extends beyond employment and now concerns the welfare and stability of numerous Indian families who have built their lives in the UAE.

title: Dead Snake Found In Food Packet Given To Pregnant Woman At Madhya Pradesh Anganwadi Centre
content: The packet, containing laddus meant as supplementary nutrition, had been given to Vachhala Bai Dhurve - a pregnant woman from the Karghat Kamthi village.

content: Counterfeit and substandard products not only create health risks but also weaken consumer confidence and impose economic costs on the country.

content: His father put him in cricket at the age of four, his coaches found him at 10, BCCI at 13. At 15 after becoming the MVP of IPL, Sooryavanshi is ready to show the world the true picture of Gen Z in Indian cricket.

content: The mother walked alongside him, repeatedly dipping her chunni in water and placing it over her son's body to shield him from the sun. The father pulled the stretcher.

content: Alex Karp argued that organisations have prioritised increasing token consumption over assessing the tangible value delivered

content: Raghava Lawrence, in a post on X, said that he would want to acknowledge the speculations amid many people reaching out to him for clarification.

content: Senior government sources said Vijay was informed that nearly Rs 102 crore was allegedly being diverted every month through unofficial "party fund" collections embedded within TASMAC operations.

content: the case relates to celebratory firing during a party on Dec 31, 2018, at a farmhouse in Fatehpur Beri in Delhi . a woman, archana Gupta, was hit by a bullet in the head and died in the hospital on Jan 3, 2019 .
